concerns.text: T007 mandates generating a 'Synthetic Control Dataset' locally to satisfy FR-008 and the pipeline. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which states 'External datasets MUST be fetched from the same canonical source on every run.' The task replaces the required real OmniDirector dataset ingestion with a local generation script, effectively weakening the FR-001 requirement to 'ingest the OmniDirector dataset' and the FR-008 requirement to validate against a subset of the *actual* dataset. The spec assumes the dataset exists; the task assumes it must be synthesized, creating a fundamental constraint violation.

T008 instructs the loader to 'load the synthetic dataset generated by T007'. This creates a closed loop where the system never ingests the real OmniDirector data. This violates FR-001 ('System MUST ingest the OmniDirector dataset') and FR-008 ('perform... on a subset of the dataset'). The task deliverable (a loader for synthetic data) falls short of the FR deliverable (a loader for the real dataset), constituting a silent relaxation of the core research requirement.

T030 implements Synthetic Control Validation using the 'locally generated subset from T007'. FR-008 requires this validation to be performed on a subset of the *OmniDirector dataset* where metric depth was randomized. If the real dataset does not contain such a subset (as implied by the spec's assumptions), the task cannot satisfy the FR by generating new data; it must either fail to find the subset or the spec must be amended to allow synthetic substitution. As written, T030 satisfies a different requirement (validating a synthetic generator) than the FR (validating the grid representation's invertibility on real data).

The Notes section contains a direct contradiction: 'CRITICAL: All data processing must use real OmniDirector data... NO fake data generation' followed immediately by 'Synthetic Data: Since no verified external source exists, T007 generates the required data locally'. This internal contradiction in the task document itself indicates a failure to preserve the constraint that the system must process real data, or a failure to explicitly document the necessary exception in the spec/plan before task generation.
